content:
# elizaos-plugins/plugin-evm Daily Update (Apr 17, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on April 17, 2026, focused on expanding network support and resolving security vulnerabilities within the broader framework. A new pull request was opened to integrate the Radius Network, while critical security patches for command injection and sandbox escapes were successfully implemented and closed.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS
*   **Network Integration**
    *   Initiated support for the Radius Network (eip155:723487) via [#29](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-evm/pull/29).

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   [#29](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-evm/pull/29): feat: add Radius Network support (eip155:723487). Status: Open.

## CLOSED ISSUES
*   **Security Vulnerability Patches**
    *   Resolved command injection risks in window management functions by implementing strict `windowId` validation [#6766](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-evm/issues/6766).
    *   Fixed a sandbox escape vulnerability related to `new Function()` prototype chain access in the browser workspace environment [#6767](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-evm/issues/6767).
*   **Release Workflow Cleanup**
    *   Closed multiple automated issue reports regarding failed alpha releases, as these were identified as noise to be handled by future workflow adjustments [#6776](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-evm/issues/6776), [#6777](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-evm/issues/6777), [#6794](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-evm/issues/6794), [#6797](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-evm/issues/6797), [#6798](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-evm/issues/6798).

## NEW ISSUES
*   None.

## ACTIVE ISSUES
*   [#6766](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-evm/issues/6766): Addressed command injection via unsanitized `windowId`. The fix involved implementing `validateWindowId()` using regex to ensure only valid IDs are processed, alongside proper escaping for window titles in AppleScript and PowerShell.
*   [#6767](https://github.com/elizaos-plugins/plugin-evm/issues/6767): Addressed sandbox escape via `new Function()`. The fix explicitly blocks `eval` in the JSDOM path and clarifies that the desktop browser workspace executes within a separate tab context, mitigating the risk of host process access.
